level_signals: 5+ years leading complex, multi-team software or technology programs (hard requirement)
Desirable: ideally 8+ years as a Technical Program Manager
Comp range $171,600 to $274,000 base, consistent with Senior/Staff TPM in Silicon Valley

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years leading complex, multi-team software or technology programs in fast-paced or early-stage environments. You get things done.
Technically strong: Comfortable working with engineers and reasoning about modern software, ML, and robotics stacks, even if you don't write code.
Systems thinking: You reason about how the parts of a complex system fit together, and how a change in one area ripples into others.
Stays neutral and adapts under pressure: Effective in high-tension, ambiguous situations; you flex your style to keep people aligned and moving.
Strong project management: Skilled at planning, prioritising, managing risk and dependencies, and adapting as things change.
Clear communicator: You explain complex ideas simply, surface risk early, listen well, and keep teams aligned.
Collaborative leader: You bring people together, influence without authority, and create a positive team culture.
Strategic thinking & problem-solving: You keep the bigger picture in mind, stay calm under pressure, and find creative solutions.
Product-minded: You focus on outcomes and the people your programs serve - prioritising by impact and defining what good looks like, not just tracking activity.
Growth mindset: Open to feedback and always looking to improve.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideally 8+ years as a Technical Program Manager.
An engineering or computer science degree, or experience working as an engineer; excels in Jira (or similar).
Exposure to autonomous vehicles, robotics, ML, simulation / evaluation, or other safety-critical or compute-heavy domains.

scope.surface: Cross-functional engineering programs spanning AI, software, robotics, infrastructure, evaluation, validation, data, and product
scope.business_model: B2B — AI software and foundation models for automakers (automated driving systems)
core_responsibilities: Drive project delivery: Own the project lifecycle and improve delivery outcomes through structured execution and coordination.
Boost efficiency: Identify bottlenecks, streamline workflows, and improve tooling and resource allocation to increase throughput.
Enable cross-team collaboration: Improve alignment across teams through effective communication, shared goals, and faster decisions.
Manage risk proactively: Anticipate issues, develop mitigation plans, and minimise impact on timelines and quality.
Keep stakeholders aligned: Engage regularly with internal stakeholders, manage expectations, and deliver on commitments.
Align with business goals: Ensure programs directly support Wayve's strategic priorities by making smart scope and resource decisions.
Champion continuous improvement: Lead retros, encourage feedback, and implement changes to improve processes, team dynamics, and outcomes.

green_flags: JD clearly identifies top 3 must-haves, reducing ambiguity for candidates
Multiple team placement options mentioned (robotics, evaluation & validation, AI platform, data operations)
Company explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every requirement
Strong mission clarity around embodied AI and autonomous driving
red_flags: Wide compensation range ($102,400 spread) may indicate significant variance in level or role scope
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; US-specific E-Verify notice linked, which may indicate no sponsorship
Office hybrid policy details not specified (no percentage or days-per-week stated)
Role placement into specific sub-team determined during process, creating some uncertainty about actual scope at application stage

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirements)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly)
location.office_time_required (hybrid stated but percentage/days not specified)
location.visa_sponsorship (not stated; E-Verify notice linked for US candidates only)
ambiguities: The JD lists 10 items under 'Essential' but explicitly states only the first three are must-haves ('The first three are our must-haves'). All 10 are treated as hard requirements here since they appear under 'Essential', but items 4-10 may be closer to strong preferences.
Visa sponsorship is neither confirmed nor denied; the E-Verify notice suggests US work authorization required but sponsorship status is unclear.
The role is described as having multiple possible team placements (robotics, evaluation & validation, AI platform, data operations); exact team determined during hiring process.
